sellItems.Name: Allow a quick sale
sellItems.Hint: Allow players to immediately exchange the found items for coins.
sellItems.Error: The game master forbade the quick sale of items.

modifier.Name: Loot cost Modifier
modifier.Hint: An additional modifier that multiplies the final dice rolls for found coins. For example, "2" will double the received coins, while "0.5" will halve them.

itemsModifier.Name: Reduce item value by half
itemsModifier.Hint: If enabled, the monetary reward for found items will be halved after applying the "Loot cost Modifier".
itemsModifier.Tooltip: The cost of the item has been reduced by half for a quick sale.

editName.Name: Editor for the name of the generated item
editName.Hint: If enabled, a window will appear when generating an item in which you can change its name.

Add.Title: Table
Add.Hint: Add one of the pre-prepared tables.

AddCustom.Title: Own table
AddCustom.Hint:
Add your own random table.
To specify the cost of items in this table, specify the following anywhere in its Description using "HTML Code":

"<p hidden id='tablePrice'>Number of rolls|Dice Type|The modifier</p>".

For example: "<p hidden id='tablePrice'>2|10|*5</p>" means that the value of the item will be determined by the roll "2d10 * 5".
AddCustom.Missing: The specified table was not found.

AddItem.Title: Subject
AddItem.Missing: The item with the specified UUID was not found.

Other.Choice: Select one of the tables of Other Items

Flaws.Hint: Specify the UUID of the item

Loot.Title: Random Loot
Loot.Hint: A quick roll on Looting preset.

Error.NotAttachedCharacter: Only for users with a assign actor.

Hovel.Name: Hovel
Hovel.Title: The hut, cottage, or hovel of a peasant.

House.Name: House
House.Title: The home of the burgeoning middle-class such as a small-time merchant, shop-keeper, tradesman, or scholar.

Estate.Name: Estate
Estate.Title: The mansion or secondary home of the richest members of society, such as wealthy merchants, gentry, or aristocrats.

WizardsHouse.Name: Wizard’s Home
WizardsHouse.Title: An established magician’s home or tower is likely to have some individual treasure.

Workshop.Name: Workshop
Workshop.Title: Any workshop will contain items specific to the trade that goes on there. In addition, some items might be kept on the property for safe-keeping.

Shrine.Name: Shrine
Shrine.Title: Small buildings or simple shelters dedicated to a minor god or long-dead local hero.

Temple.Name: Temple
Temple.Title: Depending on the locale and purpose, this might be a good bet for petty larceny. It might contain anything from golden candlesticks to the collection chest.

SmallMonster.Name: Small Monster
SmallMonster.Title: Some humanoid creatures like Goblins or Fimir have an attraction to shinies.

LargeMonster.Name: Large Monster
LargeMonster.Title: Many larger monsters are attracted to shiny or valuable objects, and have a propensity to hoard or collect treasure in secretive lairs.

ChestOpen.Name: Chest: Open
ChestOpen.Title: Lairs and strongholds (such as castles or banks) often have small caches of coins or offerings. Secure chests and vaults will be guarded with traps, locks, and soldiers safe-guarding the treasure. And possibly a Daemon or two.

ChestSecure.Name: Chest: Secure
ChestSecure.Title: Lairs and strongholds (such as castles or banks) often have small caches of coins or offerings. Secure chests and vaults will be guarded with traps, locks, and soldiers safe-guarding the treasure. And possibly a Daemon or two.

ChestVault.Name: Chest: Vault
ChestVault.Title: Lairs and strongholds (such as castles or banks) often have small caches of coins or offerings. Secure chests and vaults will be guarded with traps, locks, and soldiers safe-guarding the treasure. And possibly a Daemon or two.

Peasant.Name: Peasant
Peasant.Title: The beggars, villagers, and hard-working class which constitutes the majority of the Old World.

Citizen.Name: Citizen
Citizen.Title: A semi-affluent member of the middle-class, such as shop-keepers and small merchants.

Noble.Name: Noble
Noble.Title: An aristocrat or wealthy upper-class member of society who enjoys flaunting their wealth.

Wizard.Name: Wizard
Wizard.Title: An eccentric or powerful spellcaster might have some rare item on them.

Hereditary.Name: Hereditary
Hereditary.Title: This weapon has been passed down from father to son for generations. It is formidable and beautiful, but visibly worn.

Desecrated.Name: Desecrated
Desecrated.Title: This item was found in a place saturated with corruption. It can be dangerous both to enemies and to the owner themselves.

Mysterious.Name: Mysterious
Mysterious.Title: The item seems to possess some inner power. Or maybe not.

Heroic.Name: Heroic
Heroic.Title: This item belonged to an ancient hero, whose essence is reflected in the properties of the artifact.

Ancient.Name: Ancient
Ancient.Title: This item reeks of history. It has been gathering dust for a long time, but it clearly has potential.

Magical.Name: Magical
Magical.Title: Magical winds have been bound into this item. It is skillfully crafted and imbued with power.

Damaged.Name: Damaged
Damaged.Title: This item looks damaged. It might not be wise to use it.

Fatal.Name: Fatal
Fatal.Title: This item has caused the death of many heroes, but every time there is someone who decides to acquire it.

Insignificant.Name: Insignificant
Insignificant.Title: This item is either someone's crude mockery or deliberate sabotage. Using it is sheer suicide.
